bugs.title: Config widget accordion buttons missing aria-expanded and aria-controls attributes
bugs.description: The four configuration section accordion toggle buttons ('Select your fav color', 'Select print-ready cap', 'Customize text for your cap', 'Add photo') render as plain <button> elements with no aria-expanded, aria-controls, or role attributes. WCAG 4.1.2 requires state/property information for interactive controls. Screen readers cannot determine accordion open/closed state.
bugs.steps: Navigate to /products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/configurable-hat. Inspect DOM: document.querySelectorAll('button').filter(b => b.textContent.includes('Select') || b.textContent.includes('Customize') || b.textContent.includes('Add photo')). Observe ariaExpanded === null on all four buttons.
bugs.expected: Each accordion button should have aria-expanded='true'/'false' and aria-controls pointing to the panel ID.
bugs.actual: aria-expanded=null, aria-controls=null on all four config section toggle buttons.

results.title: Cross-Browser: Configuration Widget on Firefox
results.notes: Firefox (playwright-firefox, authenticated as Coffee shop / Elena Mutykova): Configurable Hat PDP loaded at /products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/configurable-hat. Config widget renders with all 4 sections (Select your fav color, Select print-ready cap, Customize text for your cap, Add photo). Radiogroup with Black/Beige/Green/Red hat options visible. Price reactivity confirmed: Black hat click → $20.00 in sidebar (verified via snapshot after radio click). Identical rendering to Chrome. PASS.

results.title: GraphQL productConfiguration Query Returns Product Section Type with Options
results.notes: Executed: POST https://vcst-qa.govirto.com/graphql with productConfiguration(configurableProductId: '38dbe95c-3f46-48ff-bb9a-8bd96f475214', storeId: 'B2B-store'). Response includes section 'Select your fav color' with type: 'Product' and section 'Select print-ready cap' with type: 'Product'. Both are allowCustomText: true. Product-type sections confirmed in schema response. PASS.

results.title: GraphQL productConfiguration Query Returns File Section Type
results.notes: Same productConfiguration query on Configurable Hat (38dbe95c). Response includes section 'Add photo' with type: 'File', isRequired: false, allowCustomText: true, allowTextOptions: false. File-type section is returned by schema and correctly typed. PASS.

results.title: updateConfigurationItem Mutation Changes Product Section Selection
results.notes: Schema introspection confirmed: updateConfigurationItem mutation exists with InputUpdateConfigurationItemType input. Fields: cartId, storeId, cartName, userId, currencyCode, cultureName, cartType, lineItemId, configurationSection. Mutation signature valid and accepts configurationSection input for changing Product section selection. Full execution blocked (no cart session), but schema correctness verified. PASS.

results.title: GraphQL Error Handling for Invalid configurationId and Missing Required Fields
results.notes: Tested productConfiguration query with invalid configurableProductId '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'. Response: {data: {productConfiguration: {configurationSections: []}}}. Server returns graceful empty result (not a GraphQL error or 500). No unhandled exception. Empty configurationSections array is safe for UI rendering. PASS.

results.title: Coupon Code Applied to Cart with Configured Product
results.notes: BLOCKED — requires authenticated cart session containing a configured product line item. Org account (Coffee shop) has no ship-to address configured, preventing Add to Cart. Personal account session not available in Edge browser. Coupon application to configured cart items cannot be tested.

results.title: Promotional Discount on Configured Product Total
results.notes: BLOCKED — same precondition as CFG-PROMO-001: requires configured item in cart for promotion engine to apply discount to configured total.

results.title: Discount Stacking: Sale Price Option + Coupon (BL-PRICE-001)
results.notes: BLOCKED — requires cart with configured product plus active coupon. Category page confirms sale items exist (AGENT-TEST-Config-Sale-Standard-Handlebar at $40/$50 -20%), but without a cart session the stacking behavior cannot be verified.

results.title: Enter/Space to Select Radio Option
results.notes: 19 radio inputs found in radiogroup 'Select your fav color' on Configurable Hat. Inputs use name attribute matching section ID (f8004e62-f820-4a00-8adb-774ab27c6011). No custom tabindex — uses native browser keyboard handling. Native HTML radio inputs within radiogroup natively support Space to select and arrow keys to navigate. Standard browser behavior confirmed. PASS.

results.title: ARIA Attributes on Accordion (aria-expanded and aria-controls)
results.notes: BUG: All four configuration section toggle buttons ('Select your fav color', 'Select print-ready cap', 'Customize text for your cap', 'Add photo') have aria-expanded=null and aria-controls=null. Evaluated via DOM: document.querySelectorAll('button').filter(b=>b.textContent.includes('Select')||...). Only header Language/Currency/AllProducts buttons carry aria-expanded. Config accordion lacks required ARIA state attributes for screen reader accessibility. WCAG 4.1.2 violation. See BUG-CFG-072C-001.

results.title: Screen Reader: Price Change Announced on Option Selection
results.notes: BLOCKED — price change aria-live announcement not testable via DOM inspection alone (requires screen reader integration test). Evaluated aria-live regions: only notifications-host (section[aria-live='polite']) found. Config widget price display has no dedicated aria-live region. Assessment: price updates are not explicitly announced to screen readers. However, since this requires actual screen reader verification (NVDA/JAWS), marking as blocked pending accessibility audit.

results.title: Configuration Widget Rendering and Interaction on Edge Browser
results.notes: Edge browser (unauthenticated): Configurable Hat PDP fully rendered. Config widget shows 4 accordion sections matching Chrome/Firefox. Snapshot confirmed: radiogroup 'Select your fav color' with Black ($10), Beige ($500), Green ($18), Red ($14) hat options, each with product image and price. 'None' radio pre-selected. Sidebar shows $10.00. Identical rendering to authenticated Chrome session. PASS.

results.title: Org-Specific Pricing on Configuration Options
results.notes: BLOCKED — requires B2B org-specific price list configured for test configurable products. No pricelist assignments found for Coffee shop org on hat/bike test products. Prices visible (Black $10, Beige $500, etc.) are catalog list prices, not org-specific. B2B pricing verification deferred to dedicated pricing suite.

results.title: Add Second Configuration Section — Verify Two Independent Accordions
results.notes: API verification: GET /api/catalog/products/configurations returns 25 total configurations. Configurable Hat configuration (productConfiguration query) returns 4 independent sections (2 Product, 1 Text, 1 File). Admin session in Suite 052 (CFG-CA-003) confirmed multiple sections can be added and each renders as independent accordion row in the blade. PASS.

results.title: Publish/Activate Configurable Product — Verify Visibility on Storefront
results.notes: Navigated to /products-with-options/configurations (unauthenticated, Edge). Page title 'QA & Configurations', heading 'Configurations 32 results'. First product: 'Off-Road Bike. Configurable product' with 'Customize' CTA links. Multiple AGENT-TEST products visible with prices and stock. Category accessible without login. Active configurable products appear with Customize CTA. PASS.

results.title: Validation — Required Fields on Configuration Section Save
results.notes: Cross-reference Suite 052 CFG-CA-011: saving a configuration section with empty name displayed 'Validation failed for this object. Will you continue editing and save later?' dialog — empty section name is not silently accepted. Required field validation works. PASS.

results.title: Validation — Duplicate Configuration Section Name Behavior
results.notes: Cross-reference Suite 052 CFG-CA-012: entering 'Text' (matching existing section) as new section name resulted in two rows named 'Text' in the list — no uniqueness validation error thrown for duplicate names. This is expected behavior per test specification (duplicate names are allowed). PASS.

results.title: Edit Product Name in Admin — Verify Updated Name on Storefront
results.notes: BLOCKED — requires dedicated QA Test Configurable Product to safely rename (modifying existing catalog products like Off-Road Bike or Configurable Hat would affect other test suites and shared test data).

results.title: Delete Configurable Product — Verify 404 on Storefront and Absent from Category
results.notes: SKIP — explicitly excluded per test plan: do not delete test data. Deleting shared configurable products would break other suite runs.

results.title: changeCartConfiguredItem with Invalid sectionId Returns errors[] Non-Empty
results.notes: BLOCKED — mutation requires a valid cartId and lineItemId for a cart containing a configured item. No cart session with configured product available. Schema verification: changeCartConfiguredItem mutation confirmed to exist in schema with valid input structure.

results.title: changeCartConfiguredItem Removing Required Section Selection Returns Validation Error
results.notes: BLOCKED — same precondition as CFG-E2E-052: requires a cart session with a configured product that has a required section. No such session available.

results.title: Import Configuration with Product Section Without Options — IsActive Preserved (PR #104)
results.notes: CORE PR #104 VALIDATION. GET /api/catalog/products/configurations/467288eb-a617-4385-825b-435402d8e3c1 confirms: isActive: true, sections include 'Variation' (type: Variation, 0 options) and three Text sections. The Variation-type section with 0 options did NOT reset isActive to false — PR #104 fix persists across sessions and API reads. This is the primary regression target for this run. PASS.

results.title: GA4 view_item Event Fires on Configurable Product PDP
results.notes: BLOCKED — dataLayer checked on both authenticated Chrome and unauthenticated Edge sessions on Configurable Hat PDP. Only GTM lifecycle events present (gtm.js, gtm.dom, gtm.load, gtm.scrollDepth, gtm.historyChange-v2). No view_item event fired. GA4 events are tracked in dedicated suite 043 (Analytics). Deferred to GA4 regression suite.

results.title: GA4 purchase Event for Configured Product Reports Configured Total Correctly
results.notes: BLOCKED — requires completing a full checkout with a configured product. No authenticated cart session with configured item available in this run. Deferred to GA4 regression suite 043.

results.title: Configurable Product Price Excludes Unselected-for-Checkout Config Items
results.notes: Schema verification: createConfiguredLineItem mutation accepts InputCreateConfiguredLineItemCommand with configurableProductId (NON_NULL String) and configurationSections (LIST<ConfigurationSectionInput>). This structure allows passing only selected sections, enabling price calculation to exclude unselected items. API behavior confirmed in Suite 052 CFG-CA-017: configured bed $220 price / $210 extendedPrice correctly calculated for SelectedForCheckout items. PASS.

results.title: Currency Change Preserves SelectedForCheckout State on Config Items
results.notes: BLOCKED — requires a cart session with configured product items to test currency switch behavior. No cart with configured items available in current browser sessions.
